_metadata.description: Demand pattern library for hydraulic analysis
_metadata.units: Multiplier factors (dimensionless) — multiply by base demand to get actual demand
_metadata.timestep: 1 hour (24 values = 24 hours, starting at midnight)

residential_wsaa.name: Residential (WSAA)
residential_wsaa.source: WSAA WSA 03-2011 Table 4.2 — typical residential diurnal demand pattern
residential_wsaa.description: Australian residential demand with morning and evening peaks

commercial_wsaa.name: Commercial (WSAA)
commercial_wsaa.source: WSAA WSA 03-2011 — typical commercial/office demand pattern
commercial_wsaa.description: Business hours peak with minimal overnight demand

industrial_constant.name: Industrial (Constant)
industrial_constant.source: Standard industrial assumption — 24/7 process water demand
industrial_constant.description: Near-constant demand with slight shift change variations

irrigation_night.name: Irrigation (Night-Heavy)
irrigation_night.source: Australian irrigation practice — off-peak watering to reduce evaporation
irrigation_night.description: Heavy night/early morning irrigation, minimal daytime use

hospital.name: Hospital
hospital.source: WSAA guidelines — healthcare facility demand pattern
hospital.description: Nearly flat with morning peak for sterilisation and laundry

school_term.name: School (Term Time)
school_term.source: Education facility demand — term time pattern
school_term.description: School hours demand with lunch peak, zero overnight

mining_process.name: Mining Process Water
mining_process.source: Typical mining slurry operation — 24/7 with shift-change dips
mining_process.description: Continuous process with brief reductions at shift changes (6am, 2pm, 10pm)
